$comment: ELF64 relocatable + dynamic for x86_64 (Linux / SysV). First shipped object-format JSON (plan 14 LK1, 2026-05-29). Consumed by `src/link/format/elf.cpp` via the format-blind linker engine. Section names + flags + ELF identity fields are the SAME values readelf prints; relocation `nativeId` values are the ELF type numbers stored in `r_info` low 32 bits.
$dataModelComment: FC3 c1: 64-bit linux/darwin are LP64 (int 32, long 64, ptr 64). Consumed by the semantic tier (buildCuMir threads format.dataModel() into analyze()): c-subset long binds I64 here. REQUIRED closed-enum field; the loader rejects a missing or unknown value (a silent default would bake wrong widths).
$bitFieldStrategyComment: D-CSUBSET-BITFIELD-ABI-EXACT: C bit-field allocation is OS/format-determined (the x86_64 CPU target serves BOTH this ELF-SysV format and PE-MS), so the strategy lives here, not on the target. SysV/Itanium/GNU LSB-first packing into the declared-type unit — byte-ABI-exact to gcc (verified by the conformance witness). Different-sized adjacent bit-fields may SHARE a unit (unlike MSVC).

elf.$comment: Elf64_Ehdr identity fields. `class` = ELFCLASS64; `data` = ELFDATA2LSB (little-endian); `osabi` = ELFOSABI_NONE (SysV, conventional for relocatable .o); `machine` = EM_X86_64 (62).

sections.$comment: Executable code. sh_type = SHT_PROGBITS (1); sh_flags = SHF_ALLOC | SHF_EXECINSTR (0x6); addralign = 16 (x86_64 psABI).
sections.name: .text

sections.$comment: Relocation table for .text. sh_type = SHT_RELA (4); sh_flags = SHF_INFO_LINK (0x40); addralign = 8; entrysize = 24 (Elf64_Rela). sh_link/sh_info are set per-link.
sections.name: .rela.text

sections.$comment: Symbol table. sh_type = SHT_SYMTAB (2); addralign = 8; entrysize = 24 (Elf64_Sym).
sections.name: .symtab

sections.$comment: Symbol-name string table. sh_type = SHT_STRTAB (3); addralign = 1.
sections.name: .strtab

sections.$comment: Section-header string table. sh_type = SHT_STRTAB (3); addralign = 1. e_shstrndx of the ELF header points at this section.
sections.name: .shstrtab

sections.$comment: D-LK-OBJECT-EXTERN-SYMBOL-NAMES: empty non-executable-stack marker for foreign-toolchain interop. sh_type = SHT_PROGBITS (1); sh_flags = 0 (NO SHF_EXECINSTR) is what tells `ld` the stack need not be executable; addralign = 1; size 0. The ELF writer emits it (appended last, ET_REL only) iff this row is present.
sections.name: .note.GNU-stack

relocations.$comment: PC-relative 32-bit (call / jmp rel32). ELF type R_X86_64_PC32 = 2. `kind` = 1 matches x86_64.target.json's `rel32` row.
relocations.name: R_X86_64_PC32

relocations.$comment: Absolute 64-bit. ELF type R_X86_64_64 = 1. `kind` = 2 matches x86_64.target.json's `abs64` row.
relocations.name: R_X86_64_64

relocations.$comment: Absolute 32-bit zero-extend. ELF type R_X86_64_32 = 10. `kind` = 3 matches x86_64.target.json's `abs32` row.
relocations.name: R_X86_64_32
